sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew, has been experiencing lower back pain for an extended period, with the pain radiating down his left leg.
He rates his current pain level as 6 out of 10, with a worst-case scenario of 13 out of 10.
The pain is exacerbated by standing, walking, coughing, and sneezing, and is alleviated by lying down.
He has difficulty standing and walking due to pain, and uses a cane for support.
He has numbness and tingling in his lower extremities, with weakness in both legs.
Past medical history: The patient had a fall off a roof 30 years ago, which may have resulted in a fracture or possible disk injury.
He has been experiencing back pain for about a month.
Review of systems: The patient's symptoms are primarily localized to his lower back and left leg, with no mention of other systemic symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's back exam revealed no bony abnormalities, redness, or bruising.
He was positive for pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and experienced pain with flexion and extension of his back.
His strength in the left leg was 4 out of 5, and 5 out of 5 in the right leg.
His reflexes were brisk and motor and sensory function was intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: The MRI showed a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae associated with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement, causing lower back pain and radiating pain down his left leg.
Problem list: Back pain; acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement; lower back pain; leg pain; weakness in both legs.
Plan:
I recommended an epidural steroid injection to alleviate the inflammation and pain.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
The patient will see me again in a month to assess the effectiveness of the treatment.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in a month to review the effectiveness of the treatment and adjust as needed.
